items.title: 34% of the S&P 500's Value Comes From Just 8 Stocks
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The S&P 500 index has become highly concentrated, with just 8 stocks making up over 34% of its value. This raises concerns about the market's vulnerability to pullbacks in these 'Fateful Eight' stocks, which have outperformed the broader market. Investors may want to consider equal-weight funds to diversify their exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Alphabet is one of the 'Fateful Eight' stocks that have outperformed the broader market, suggesting it is a key driver of the S&P 500's recent gains.

Broadcom has recently surpassed a $1 trillion market cap, joining the 'Fateful Eight' stocks that have outperformed the broader market, suggesting it is a key driver of the S&P 500's recent gains.

The article suggests that the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF could be a good option for investors concerned about the concentration risks in the S&P 500, as it would be more resilient to a pullback in the 'Fateful Eight' stocks.
